prompt: I earn £67,000 from my job. This year I received £3,400 in dividends from shares in a general investment account, £950 in savings interest, and I have a SIP through work with £3,600 in free shares (held 3 years) and £1,800 in partnership shares. I sold some shares from the GIA for a £2,800 gain. I also put £10,000 into my stocks and shares ISA. Should I be thinking about moving more into my ISA? What's my tax bill looking like?

assertions.text: Identifies user as higher rate taxpayer (40%)
Applies dividend allowance (£500) and taxes excess at 33.75%
Applies PSA correctly (£500 for higher rate) to savings interest
Explains SIP free shares held 3 years: partial income tax applies on sale
Explains SIP partnership shares bought from pre-tax salary
Applies CGT annual exempt amount (£3,000) to the £2,800 gain — no CGT due
Confirms ISA income/gains are completely tax-free and not reportable
Recommends bed and ISA strategy to shelter GIA holdings
Provides numerical tax calculations with workings
